articles.title: Breaking Tech News on March 28, 2026: AI Revolution, Hardware Upgrades, and Security Threats
articles.summary: Comprehensive coverage of major tech developments including Lucid Software's MCP server enhancements, Revenium's AI Outcomes ROI measurement tool, Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live conversational AI, and significant hardware releases like AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 processor with 208MB cache.
key_points: Lucid Software introduces Process Agent to accelerate team collaborations on complex diagrams
Revenium launches AI Outcomes for measuring ROI at workflow level
Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live enables real-time audio chats with visual context integration
AMD unveils Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition with 208MB cache for enhanced gaming performance
Security concerns raised about leaked iPhone hacking tools Coruna and DarkSword

articles.title: How AI giants tried to storm the last stronghold of the human mind: the math olympiads
articles.summary: Investigation reveals controversy around AI companies' participation in the 2025 International Mathematical Olympiad, where OpenAI claimed 'gold medal-level performance' without official evaluation, causing tension between human competitors and tech corporations.
key_points: OpenAI announced gold medal-level performance without official IMO evaluation
Google DeepMind also participated but faced criticism for pressuring volunteer graders
Controversy sparked debate about separate competitions for AI vs human participants
Terence Tao suggests different format needed for AI competitions
Chinese company DeepSeek later claimed similar achievements without coordination

articles.title: Tech companies leaning hard into the US defense industry
articles.summary: Major tech companies including Palantir, Anthropic, and Anduril are increasingly collaborating with the US Department of Defense, marking a significant shift from previous resistance to military contracts.
key_points: Palantir's Maven platform extensively used in offensive operations against Iran
Anduril secured $20 billion defense contract for drone defense systems
Anthropic's AI systems used in Middle East conflicts despite Pentagon restrictions
Record $49 billion private equity investment in defense technology companies
Shift driven by Ukraine conflict and concerns about China's ambitions toward Taiwan

articles.title: Smaller Is Better in Silicon Valley's 'Tiny Team' Moment
articles.summary: Silicon Valley embraces minimal team structures with some companies operating on 'two-slice teams' consisting of just one person plus AI tools, challenging traditional notions of organizational scale.
key_points: Rise of 'tiny teams' with high revenue-to-worker ratios
Dan Shipper's 'two-slice team' concept: one person plus AI tools
Evolution from Jeff Bezos' 'two pizza rule' to minimal staffing
AI enables small startups to compete with large companies' capabilities
Focus on 'high agency' employees who accomplish more with AI assistance

articles.title: Latest Technology News and Developments
articles.summary: Reuters provides ongoing coverage of technology sector developments including digital trade rules, tech industry policy changes, and international technology cooperation.
key_points: WTO members introduce world's first baseline digital trade rules
Ongoing coverage of technology policy and regulation
International technology cooperation and standards development
Tech industry response to global economic conditions
Emerging technology trends and market analysis
